awesomeListMeta.descriptionHTML: A curated list of awesome R packages, frameworks and software.
awesomeListMeta.description: A curated list of awesome R packages, frameworks and software.

nodes.itemHTML: • golem - A framework for building production-grade Shiny apps.
• httpgdstars232 - Asynchronous http server graphics device for R.
• corporaexplorer - Dynamic exploration of text collections
• arrow - An interface to the Arrow C++ library.
• feather stars2.6k - Fast, interoperable binary data frame storage for Python, R, and more powered by Apache Arrow.
• fst - Lightning Fast Serialization of Data Frames for R.
• havenstars384 - Improved methods to import SPSS, Stata and SAS files in R.
• jsonlitestars330 - A robust and quick way to parse JSON files in R.
• qsstars324 - Quick serialization of R objects.
• readxl - Read excel files (.xls and .xlsx) into R.
• readr stars923 - A fast and friendly way to read tabular data into R.
• riostars534 - A Swiss-Army Knife for Data I/O.
• readODSstars46 - Read OpenDocument Spreadsheets into R as data.frames.
• RcppTOMLstars25 - Rcpp Bindings to C++ parser for TOML files.
• vroomstars570 - Fast reading of delimited files.
• writexl - Portable, light-weight data frame to xlsx exporter for R.
• yamlstars137 - R package for converting objects to and from YAML.
• lightgbm - Light Gradient Boosting Machine.
• torch - Tensors and Neural Networks with 'GPU' Acceleration.
• reticulate - Interface to 'Python'.
• cpp11stars171 - cpp11 is a header-only R package that helps R package developers handle R objects with C++ code. It's similar to Rcpp but with different design trade-offs and features.
• Posts - Great R blog posts or Rticles.
• RCOBOLDIstars10 - Import COBOL CopyBook data files directly into R as properly structured data frames. Package builds are available via Dratstars0 and DockerHub.
• VSCode - vscode-R + vscode-r-lsp VSCode R Langauage Support
• VSCode - vscode-R + vscode-r-lsp VSCode R Langauage Support
• gtstars1.6k - Easily generate information-rich, publication-quality tables from R
• reduxstars77 - Redis client for R.
• radian stars1.5k (formerly rtichoke) - A modern R console with syntax highlighting.
• fablestars492 - univariate and multivariate time series forecasting models
• r2d3 - R Interface to D3 Visualizations
• rstats-edstars434 - List of courses teaching R
• promises - Abstractions for Promise-Based Asynchronous Programming
• tinytex - A lightweight and easy-to-maintain LaTeX distribution
• Readings in Applied Data Sciencestars1.6k - These readings reflect Hadley's personal thoughts about applied data science.
• prophetstars15k - Tool for producing high quality forecasts for time series data that has multiple seasonality with linear or non-linear growth.
• tidyversestars1.3k - Easily install and load packages from the tidyverse
• purrrstars1.1k - A functional programming toolkit for R
• hrbrthemesstars1.1k - 🔏 Opinionated, typographic-centric ggplot2 themes and theme components
• xaringanstars1.4k - Create HTML5 slides with R Markdown and the JavaScript library
• blogdownstars1.6k - Create Blogs and Websites with R Markdown
• gluestars619 - Glue strings to data in R. Small, fast, dependency free interpreted string literals.
• covrstars307 - Test coverage reports for R
• lintrstars1k - Static Code Analysis for R
• reprexstars687 - Render bits of R code for sharing, e.g., on GitHub or StackOverflow.
• reticulatestars1.5k - R Interface to Python
• tensorflowstars1.3k - TensorFlow for R
• utf8stars106 - Manipulating and printing UTF-8 text that fixes multiple bugs in R's UTF-8 handling.
• Patchworkstars2.1k - Combine separate ggplots into the same graphic.
• RStudio - A powerful and productive user interface for R. Works great on Windows, Mac, and Linux.
• IRkernel stars1.5k - R kernel for Jupyter.
• magrittr stars907 - Let's pipe it.
• dplyr stars4.2k - Fast data frames manipulation and database query.
• data.table stars3.1k - Fast data manipulation in a short and flexible syntax.
• reshape2 stars204 - Flexible rearrange, reshape and aggregate data.
• broom stars14 - Convert statistical analysis objects into tidy data frames.
• stringi stars258 - ICU based string processing package.
• stringr stars485 - Consistent API for string processing, built on top of stringi.
• ggplot2 stars5.6k - An implementation of the Grammar of Graphics.
• DiagrammeR stars1.6k - Create JS graph diagrams and flowcharts in R.
• formattable stars676 - Formattable Data Structures.
• ggvis stars703 - Interactive grammar of graphics for R.
• plotly stars2.3k - Interactive ggplot2 and Shiny plotting with plot.ly.
• rCharts stars1.2k - Interactive JS Charts from R.
• knitr stars2.2k - Easy dynamic report generation in R.
• rmarkdown - Dynamic documents for R.
• slidify stars849 - Generate reproducible html5 slides from R markdown.
• shiny stars4.8k - Easy interactive web applications with R. See also awesome-rshinystars673
• httr stars959 - User-friendly RCurl wrapper.
• XML - Tools for parsing and generating XML within R.
• xml2 - Optimized tools for parsing and generating XML within R.
• rvest stars1.4k - Simple web scraping for R, using CSSSelect or XPath syntax.
• OpenCPU - HTTP API for R handling concurrent calls, based on the Apache2 web server, to expose R code as REST web services and create full-sized, multi-page web applications.
• foreach - Executing the loop in parallel.
• future - A minimal, efficient, cross-platform unified Future API for parallel and distributed processing in R; designed for beginners as well as advanced developers.
• SparkR stars644 - R frontend for Spark.
• Rcpp - Rcpp provides a powerful API on top of R, make function in R extremely faster.
• AnomalyDetection stars3.5k - AnomalyDetection R package from Twitter.
• rstan - R interface to the Stan MCMC software.
• quantmod - Quantitative Financial Modelling & Trading Framework for R.
• zoo - S3 Infrastructure for Regular and Irregular Time Series.
• Bioconductor - Tools for the analysis and comprehension of high-throughput genomic data.
• igraph - A collection of network analysis tools.
• devtools stars2.2k - Tools to make an R developer's life easier.
• testthat stars810 - An R package to make testing fun.
• R6 stars369 - simpler, faster, lighter-weight alternative to R's built-in classes.
• pryr stars196 - Make it easier to understand what's going on in R.
• roxygen stars521 - Describe your functions in comments next to their definitions.
• Rocker - R configurations for Docker.
• swirl - An interactive R tutorial directly in your R console.
• DataScienceR stars1.8k - a list of R tutorials for Data Science, NLP and Machine Learning.
• Advanced R, 2nd ed. by Hadley Wickham (2019) - An online version of the Advanced R book.
• tmapstars682 - R package for thematic maps
• wbstats - Tools for searching and downloading data and statistics from the World Bank Data API and the World Bank Data Catalog API.
• kable - Build fancy HTML or 'LaTeX' tables using 'kable()' from 'knitr'.
• idendrostars8 - interactive exploration of dendrograms (trees of hierarchical clustering).
• texregstars99 - Formatting statistical models in LaTex and HTML.
• An Introduction to R - A very good introductory text on R, also covers some advanced topic. See also the Manuals section on CRAN
• CRAN Contributed Docs - CRAN Contributed Documentation in many languages.
• Quick-R - An excellent quick reference
• CRAN Task Views - Task Views for CRAN packages.
• R Weekly - Weekly updates about R and Data Science. R Weekly is openly developed on GitHub.
• R Bloggers - There are people scattered across the Web who blog about R. This is simply an aggregator of many of those feeds.
• R-users - A job board for R users (and the people who are looking to hire them)
• R for Data Science by Garrett Grolemund & Hadley Wickham - Free book from RStudio developers with emphasis on data science workflow.
• R Cookbook by Winston Chang - A problem-oriented online book that supports his R Graphics Cookbook, 2nd ed. (2018).
• R Packages, 2nd ed. by Hadley Wickham & Jennifer Bryan - A book (in paper and website formats) on writing R packages.
• R for SAS and SPSS users by Bob Muenchen (2012) - An excellent resource for users already familiar with SAS or SPSS.

• Introduction to Statistical Learning with Application in R by Gareth James et al. (2017) - A simplified and "operational" version of The Elements of Statistical Learning. Free softcopy provided by its authors.
• The R Inferno by Patrick Burns (2011) - Patrick Burns gives insight into R's ins and outs along with its quirks!
• Efficient R Programming by Colin Gillespie & Robin Lovelace (2017) - An online version of the O’Reilly book: Efficient R Programming.
• The R Programming Wikibook - A collaborative handbook for R.
• The Art of R Programming - It's a good resource for systematically learning fundamentals such as types of objects, control statements, variable scope, classes and debugging in R.
• R Cookbook, 2nd ed. by JD Long & Paul Teetor (2019) - A quick and simple introduction to conducting many common statistical tasks with R.

• Use R! Series by Springer - This series of inexpensive and focused books from Springer publish shorter books aimed at practitioners. Books can discuss the use of R in a particular subject area, such as Bayesian networks, ggplot2 and Rcpp.
• R Books Liststars189 - List of R Books.
• Readings in Applied Data Sciencestars1.6k - These readings reflect Hadley's personal thoughts about applied data science.
• tidygraphstars488 - A tidy API for graph manipulation
• ggforcestars812 - ggplot2 extension framework
• rayshaderstars1.6k - 2D and 3D data visualizations via rgl
• ICONstars6 - complex systems & networks datasets from the Index of COmplex Networks (ICON) database webpage.
• echarts4rstars511 - R wrapper to Echarts version 4
• Microsoft R - Revolution R would be offered free to academic users and commercial software would focus on big data, large scale multiprocessor functionality.
• ggstatsplotstars1.6k - ggplot2 Based Plots with Statistical Details
• redocstars497 - Reversible Reproducible Documents
• pedquant - Public Economic Data and Quantitative Analysis
• rOpenGov Open government data, computational social science, digital humanities
• rOpenHealth Public health data
• rOpenSci Open science
• RDocumentation - Search through all CRAN, Bioconductor, Github packages and their archives with RDocumentation.
• rdrr.io - Find R package documentation. Try R packages in your browser.
• rnotebook.io - Create online R Jupyter Notebooks for free.
• Conda - Most R packages are available through the Conda polyglot cross-platform dependency manager.
• DataExplorerstars449 - Fast exploratory data analysis with minimum code.
• snakecasestars129 - Automatically parse and convert strings into cases like snake or camel among others.
• R Suite - A package to design flexible and reproducible deployment workflows for R.
• vroomstars570 - Fast reading of delimited files
• GWmodel - Geographically-Weighted Models
• scorecardstars140 - Credit Risk Scorecard
• plot3D - Plotting Multi-Dimensional Data
• plot3Drgl - Plotting Multi-Dimensional Data - Using 'rgl'
• officer - An R package to generate Microsoft Word, Microsoft PowerPoint and HTML reports.

• flextable - An R package to embed complex tables (merged cells, multi-level headers and footers, conditional formatting) in Microsoft Word, Microsoft PowerPoint and HTML reports. It cooperates with the [officer] package and integrates with [rmarkdown] reports.
• lme4stars527 - Generalized mixed-effects models.
• nlme - Mixed-effects models, handling user-specified matrix of residual covariance, relevant for the anaysis of repeated observations in longitudinal trials.
• glmmTMB - Generalized mixed-effects models, handling user-specified matrix of residual covariance, relevant for the anaysis of repeated observations in longitudinal trials.
• Bio7 - An IDE contains tools for model creation, scientific image analysis and statistical analysis for ecological modelling.
• RCassandra - Direct interface (not Java) to the most basic functionality of Apache Cassandra.
• gapminderstars253 - Excerpt from the Gapminder dataset (data about countries through the past 50 years).
• RPostgresstars294 - an DBI-compliant interface to the postgres database.
• anomalizestars313 - Tidy Anomaly Detection using Twitter's AnomalyDetection method.
• Jamovi and JASP - Desktop software for both Bayesian and Frequentist methods, using a UI familiar to SPSS users.
• sf - Improved Classes and Methods for Spatial Data.
• sinewstars157 - Generate roxygen2 skeletons populated with information scraped from the function script.
• curlstars186 - A Modern and Flexible Web Client for R.
• heatmaplystars322 - Interactive heatmaps with D3.
• d3heatmapstars238 - Interactive heatmaps with D3 (no longer maintained).
• odbcstars329 - Connect to ODBC databases (using the DBI interface)
• Patchworkstars2.1k - Combine separate ggplots into the same graphic.
• lubridatestars656 - A set of functions to work with dates and times.
• bigmemorystars116 - Shared memory and memory-mapped matrices. The big* packages provide additional tools including linear models (biglm) and Random Forests (bigrfstars92).
• latticestars55 - A powerful and elegant high-level data visualization system.
• animationstars194 - A simple way to produce animated graphics in R, using ImageMagick.
• tinytexstars805 - A lightweight and easy-to-maintain LaTeX distribution
• wafflestars707 - 🍁 Make waffle (square pie) charts in R.
• dendextendstars138 - visualizing, adjusting and comparing trees of hierarchical clustering.
• RKWard - An extensible IDE/GUI for R.
• r2d3 - R Interface to D3 Visualizations
• promises - Abstractions for Promise-Based Asynchronous Programming
• RStudio Cheat Sheets
• drakestars1.3k - An rOpenSci package for reproducible data science workflows too big for knitr.

• tigrisstars275 - Download and use Census TIGER/Line shapefiles in R
• Vim-Rstars123 - Vim plugin for R.
• brew - Pre-compute data to enhance your report templates. Can be combined with knitr.

• Books written as part of the Johns Hopkins Data Science Specialization:
    • Exploratory Data Analysis with R by Roger D. Peng (2016) - Basic analytical skills for all sorts of data in R.
    • R Programming for Data Science by Roger D. Peng (2019) - More advanced data analysis that relies on R programming.
    • Report Writing for Data Science in R by Roger D. Peng (2019) - R-based methods for reproducible research and report generation.

• R in Action - This book aims at all levels of users, with sections for beginning, intermediate and advanced R ranging from "Exploring R data structures" to running regressions and conducting factor analyses.
• Deducer - A Menu driven data analysis GUI with a spreadsheet like data editor.
